moduleDescription: Floating-point environment. Handling of floating-point rounding and exceptions (overflow, division by zero, etc.). The types, vars and procs are bindings for the C standard library <fenv.h> header.

entries.name: FE_DIVBYZERO
entries.description: division by zero

entries.name: FE_INEXACT
entries.description: inexact result

entries.name: FE_INVALID
entries.description: invalid operation

entries.name: FE_OVERFLOW
entries.description: result not representable due to overflow

entries.name: FE_UNDERFLOW
entries.description: result not representable due to underflow

entries.name: FE_ALL_EXCEPT
entries.description: bitwise OR of all supported exceptions

entries.name: FE_DOWNWARD
entries.description: round toward -Inf

entries.name: FE_TONEAREST
entries.description: round to nearest

entries.name: FE_TOWARDZERO
entries.description: round toward 0

entries.name: FE_UPWARD
entries.description: round toward +Inf

entries.name: FE_DFL_ENV
entries.description: macro of type pointer to fenv_t to be used as the argument to functions taking an argument of type fenv_t; in this case the default environment will be used

entries.name: Tfenv
entries.description: Represents the entire floating-point environment. The floating-point environment refers collectively to any floating-point status flags and control modes supported by the implementation.

entries.name: Tfexcept
entries.description: Represents the floating-point status flags collectively, including any status the implementation associates with the flags. A floating-point status flag is a system variable whose value is set (but never cleared) when a floating-point exception is raised, which occurs as a side effect of exceptional floating-point arithmetic to provide auxiliary information. A floating-point control mode is a system variable whose value may be set by the user to affect the subsequent behavior of floating-point arithmetic.

entries.name: feclearexcept
entries.code:
proc feclearexcept(excepts: cint): cint {.importc, header: "<fenv.h>",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Clear the supported exceptions represented by excepts.

entries.name: fegetexceptflag
entries.code:
proc fegetexceptflag(flagp: ptr Tfexcept; excepts: cint): cint {.importc,
    header: "<fenv.h>", raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Store implementation-defined representation of the exception flags indicated by excepts in the object pointed to by flagp.

entries.name: feraiseexcept
entries.code:
proc feraiseexcept(excepts: cint): cint {.importc, header: "<fenv.h>",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Raise the supported exceptions represented by excepts.

entries.name: fesetexceptflag
entries.code:
proc fesetexceptflag(flagp: ptr Tfexcept; excepts: cint): cint {.importc,
    header: "<fenv.h>", raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Set complete status for exceptions indicated by excepts according to the representation in the object pointed to by flagp.

entries.name: fetestexcept
entries.code:
proc fetestexcept(excepts: cint): cint {.importc, header: "<fenv.h>",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Determine which of subset of the exceptions specified by excepts are currently set.

entries.name: fegetround
entries.code:
proc fegetround(): cint {.importc, header: "<fenv.h>", raises: [], tags: [],
                          forbids: [].}
entries.description: Get current rounding direction.

entries.name: fesetround
entries.code:
proc fesetround(roundingDirection: cint): cint {.importc, header: "<fenv.h>",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Establish the rounding direction represented by roundingDirection.

entries.name: fegetenv
entries.code:
proc fegetenv(envp: ptr Tfenv): cint {.importc, header: "<fenv.h>", raises: [],
                                       tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Store the current floating-point environment in the object pointed to by envp.

entries.name: feholdexcept
entries.code:
proc feholdexcept(envp: ptr Tfenv): cint {.importc, header: "<fenv.h>",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Save the current environment in the object pointed to by envp, clear exception flags and install a non-stop mode (if available) for all exceptions.

entries.name: fesetenv
entries.code:
proc fesetenv(a1: ptr Tfenv): cint {.importc, header: "<fenv.h>", raises: [],
                                     tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Establish the floating-point environment represented by the object pointed to by envp.

entries.name: feupdateenv
entries.code:
proc feupdateenv(envp: ptr Tfenv): cint {.importc, header: "<fenv.h>",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Save current exceptions in temporary storage, install environment represented by object pointed to by envp and raise exceptions according to saved exceptions.

entries.name: fpRadix
entries.description: The (integer) value of the radix used to represent any floating point type on the architecture used to build the program.

entries.name: mantissaDigits
entries.description: Number of digits (in base floatingPointRadix) in the mantissa of 32-bit floating-point numbers.

entries.name: digits
entries.description: Number of decimal digits that can be represented in a 32-bit floating-point type without losing precision.

entries.name: minExponent
entries.description: Minimum (negative) exponent for 32-bit floating-point numbers.

entries.name: maxExponent
entries.description: Maximum (positive) exponent for 32-bit floating-point numbers.

entries.name: min10Exponent
entries.description: Minimum (negative) exponent in base 10 for 32-bit floating-point numbers.

entries.name: max10Exponent
entries.description: Maximum (positive) exponent in base 10 for 32-bit floating-point numbers.

entries.name: minimumPositiveValue
entries.description: The smallest positive (nonzero) number that can be represented in a 32-bit floating-point type.

entries.name: maximumPositiveValue
entries.description: The largest positive number that can be represented in a 32-bit floating-point type.

entries.name: epsilon
entries.description: The difference between 1.0 and the smallest number greater than 1.0 that can be represented in a 32-bit floating-point type.

entries.name: mantissaDigits
entries.description: Number of digits (in base floatingPointRadix) in the mantissa of 64-bit floating-point numbers.

entries.name: digits
entries.description: Number of decimal digits that can be represented in a 64-bit floating-point type without losing precision.

entries.name: minExponent
entries.description: Minimum (negative) exponent for 64-bit floating-point numbers.

entries.name: maxExponent
entries.description: Maximum (positive) exponent for 64-bit floating-point numbers.

entries.name: min10Exponent
entries.description: Minimum (negative) exponent in base 10 for 64-bit floating-point numbers.

entries.name: max10Exponent
entries.description: Maximum (positive) exponent in base 10 for 64-bit floating-point numbers.

entries.name: minimumPositiveValue
entries.description: The smallest positive (nonzero) number that can be represented in a 64-bit floating-point type.

entries.name: maximumPositiveValue
entries.description: The largest positive number that can be represented in a 64-bit floating-point type.

entries.name: epsilon
entries.description: The difference between 1.0 and the smallest number greater than 1.0 that can be represented in a 64-bit floating-point type.
